blocks:
Sūrat al-Wāqiʿah

Makkan, and it is 96 verses

(١) إِذا وَقَعَتِ الْواقِعَةُ ** (1) When the Occurrence occurs, When the Resurrection (القيامة) occurs. He named it the Occurrence (واقعة) because of the certainty of its occurrence. (٢) لَيْسَ لِوَقْعَتِها كاذِبَةٌ ** (2) there is no denying its occurrence. A denying soul (نفس كاذبة). Al-Qummi said: The Resurrection (القيامة) is truth (حقّ). (٣) خافِضَةٌ ** (3) abasing, He said: [It abases] the enemies of Allah. رافِعَةٌ ** (exalting) He said: [It exalts] the friends (أولياء) of Allah. And in al-Khisal, narrated from al-Sajjad (peace be upon him): إِذا وَقَعَتِ الْواقِعَةُ ** (When the Occurrence occurs) means the Resurrection (القيامة). خافِضَةٌ ** (abasing) By Allah, it abases the enemies of Allah to the Fire (النّار). رافِعَةٌ ** (exalting) By Allah, it exalts the friends (أولياء) of Allah to Paradise (الجنّة).

(٤) إِذا رُجَّتِ الْأَرْضُ رَجًّا ** (4) when the earth is rocked with a rocking, It is moved with a severe movement (تَحْرِيكاً شَدِيداً). Al-Qummi said: Its parts will crush against one another. (٥) وَبُسَّتِ الْجِبالُ بَسًّا ** (5) and the mountains are pulverized with a pulverizing, He said: The mountains are uprooted with an uprooting (قَلْعاً). And it was said: They are crumbled like moistened sawīq (السويق الملتوت). (٦) فَكانَتْ هَباءً مُنْبَثًّا ** (6) and become scattered dust, meaning: scattered dust (غُبَاراً مُنْتَشِراً). Al-Qummi said: Al-Habāʾ (الهباء) is that which enters the niche (الكّوة) from the rays of the sun. (٧) وَكُنْتُمْ أَزْواجاً ** (7) and you become three kinds. means: categories (أَصْنَافاً). ** ثَلاثَةً ** He said: on the Day of Resurrection. (٨) فَأَصْحابُ الْمَيْمَنَةِ ما أَصْحابُ الْمَيْمَنَةِ ** (8) The People of the Right Hand—who are the People of the Right Hand? He said: They are the believers among the people burdened with responsibilities (أَصْحَابُ التَّبِعَات); they will be made to stand for the reckoning. (٩) وَأَصْحابُ الْمَشْئَمَةِ ما أَصْحابُ الْمَشْئَمَةِ. (9) And the People of the Left Hand—who are the People of the Left Hand? (١٠) وَالسَّابِقُونَ السَّابِقُونَ ** (10) And the Foremost are the foremost: It was said: They are those who preceded others to Paradise without reckoning. (١١) أُولئِكَ الْمُقَرَّبُونَ. (11) they are the ones brought near [to Allah], (١٢) فِي جَنَّاتِ النَّعِيمِ. (12) in the gardens of bliss. In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Indeed, Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, created creation in three categories, and this is His saying, the Mighty and Majestic: ** وَكُنْتُمْ أَزْواجاً ثَلاثَةً ** (and you become three kinds) and the following verses. He said: The Foremost (السَّابِقُونَ) are the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and the select ones (خَاصَّة) of Allah from His creation. He placed five spirits (أَرْوَاح) in them: He supported them with the Spirit of Holiness (رُوح الْقُدُس), by which they knew things; He supported them with the Spirit of Faith (رُوح الْإِيمَان), by which they feared Allah, the Mighty and Majestic; He supported them with the Spirit of Strength (رُوح الْقُوَّة), by which they were able to obey Allah; He supported them with the Spirit of Desire (رُوح الشَّهْوَة), by which they desired the obedience of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, and disliked His disobedience; and He placed in them the Spirit of Movement (رُوح الْمُدْرَج), by which people go and come. And He placed in the believers, the People of the Right Hand (أَصْحَابُ الْمَيْمَنَة), the Spirit of Faith (رُوح الْإِيمَان), by which they feared Allah; and He placed in them the Spirit of Strength (رُوح الْقُوَّة), by which they were strong enough for the obedience of Allah; and He placed in them the Spirit of Desire (رُوح الشَّهْوَة), by which they desired the obedience of Allah; and He placed in them the Spirit of Movement (رُوح الْمُدْرَج), by which people go and come.

In al-Amali, narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: He was asked about this verse, and He said: Gabriel said to me that this refers to Ali and his followers (شِيعَتُهُ). They are ** السَّابِقُونَ ** (the Foremost) to Paradise, ** الْمُقَرَّبُونَ ** (the ones brought near) to Allah by His generosity (بِكَرَامَتِهِ).

In al-Khisal, narrated from Ali (peace be upon him), he said: ** وَالسَّابِقُونَ السَّابِقُونَ أُولئِكَ الْمُقَرَّبُونَ ** (And the Foremost are the foremost: they are the ones brought near [to Allah]) was revealed concerning me.

In al-Ikmal, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him), in a hadith: And we are ** السَّابِقُونَ السَّابِقُونَ ** (the Foremost are the foremost), and we are the last ones (الْآخِرُونَ).

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: My father said to some people from the followers (الشِّيعَة): "You are the followers (شِيعَة) of Allah, and you are the helpers (أَنْصَار) of Allah, and you are the first Foremost (السَّابِقُونَ الْأَوَّلُونَ) and the last Foremost (السَّابِقُونَ الْآخِرُونَ), and the Foremost in this world (الدُّنْيَا) to our guardianship (وِلَايَتِنَا), and the Foremost in the Hereafter (الْآخِرَة) to Paradise."

In al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): ** السَّابِقُونَ السَّابِقُونَ ** (And the Foremost are the foremost) are four: the slain son of Adam (ابْن آدَم الْمَقْتُول), the foremost of the community (أُمَّة) of Moses (peace be upon him), who is the believer from the family of Pharaoh (مُؤْمِن آل فِرْعَوْن), the foremost of the community (أُمَّة) of Jesus (peace be upon him), who is Habib al-Najjar (حَبِيب النَّجَّار), and the foremost in the community (أُمَّة) of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, who is Ali ibn Abi Talib (peace be upon him).

(١٣) ثُلَّةٌ مِنَ الْأَوَّلِينَ ** (13) A multitude of the former generations, Meaning, they are a great number (كثير) from the former generations, meaning the past nations from the time of Adam (peace be upon him) up to Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله. (١٤) وَقَلِيلٌ مِنَ الْآخِرِينَ ** (14) and a few from the latter generations. Meaning, the Ummah of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله. (١٥) عَلى سُرُرٍ مَوْضُونَةٍ ** (15) on studded couches, Woven with gold, interlaced with pearls (الدرّ) and rubies (الياقوت). (١٦) مُتَّكِئِينَ عَلَيْها مُتَقابِلِينَ. (16) reclining on them, facing one another. (١٧) يَطُوفُ عَلَيْهِمْ ** (17) Immortal youths will go around attending to them, For service, وِلْدانٌ مُخَلَّدُونَ ** (immortal youths). It was said (قيل): meaning, they are kept forever in the form and freshness of youths (الولدان). And according to al-Qummi (القمّيّ): meaning, they are adorned with bracelets (مسوّرون). And in al-Majma', narrated from Ali عليه السلام: They are the children of the people of the world (أولاد أهل الدنيا). And narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله: He was asked about the children of the polytheists (أطفال المشركين), and He said: They are the servants of the people of Paradise (خدم أهل الجنّة).

(١٨) بِأَكْوابٍ وَأَبارِيقَ ** (with goblets and ewers) Al-Kūb (الكوب) is a vessel that has neither a handle (عروة) nor a spout (خرطوم), and al-Ibrīq (الإبريق) is a vessel that has those. وَكَأْسٍ مِنْ مَعِينٍ (a cup of flowing wine) means wine (خمر). (١٩) لا يُصَدَّعُونَ عَنْها ** (They will not get a headache from it) due to intoxication (لخمار) وَلا يُنْزِفُونَ ** (nor will they be intoxicated) meaning their minds (عقولهم) will not be drained (ينزف), or their drink will not be exhausted (ينفذ). And it was recited with the kasrah on the zāy (يُنْزِفُونَ). (٢٠) وَفاكِهَةٍ مِمَّا يَتَخَيَّرُونَ ** (and fruits of whatever they choose) meaning they select (يختارون). (٢١) وَلَحْمِ طَيْرٍ مِمَّا يَشْتَهُونَ ** (and flesh of fowl of whatever they desire) they wish for (يتمنّون). In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said: "The master of the relishes (إدام) of Paradise is meat (اللّحم)." And in a narration: "Meat (اللّحم) is the master of food in this world and the Hereafter."

(٢٢) وَحُورٌ عِينٌ ** (22) And wide-eyed houris, And it was recited (وقرئ) with al-jarr (the genitive case). (٢٣) كَأَمْثالِ اللُّؤْلُؤِ الْمَكْنُونِ ** (23) like preserved pearls, [They are] protected (المصون) from that which affects them, in purity (الصّفاء) and cleanliness (النّقاء). (٢٤) جَزاءً بِما كانُوا يَعْمَلُونَ ** (24) a reward for what they used to do. Meaning: All of that is done for them as a reward for their deeds. (٢٥) لا يَسْمَعُونَ فِيها لَغْواً ** (25) They will hear therein neither vain talk [Meaning] falsehood (باطلاً), وَلا تَأْثِيماً ** nor sinful speech, Nor attribution to sin (الإثم). Al-Qummi said: [It means] obscenity (الفحش), lies (الكذب), and singing (الغناء). (٢٦) إِلَّا قِيلاً ** (26) but only the saying: [Meaning] speech (قولاً), سَلاماً سَلاماً ** 'Peace! Peace!' The peace (السلام) will be widespread among them. (٢٧) وَأَصْحابُ الْيَمِينِ ما أَصْحابُ الْيَمِينِ. (27) And the People of the Right Hand—who are the People of the Right Hand? Al-Qummi said: الْيَمِينِ ** (the Right Hand) is the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), and His companions (أصحابه) are His followers (شيعته). (٢٨) فِي سِدْرٍ مَخْضُودٍ ** (28) amid thornless lote trees, [Meaning] whose thorns are cut off (مقطوع الشّوك). Al-Qummi said: [It is] a tree that has neither leaves (ورق) nor thorns (شوك) in it. (٢٩) وَطَلْحٍ مَنْضُودٍ ** (29) and clustered banana trees, And [it is] a banana tree (شجر موز) or Umm Ghaylan (أمّ غيلان) (1), whose fruit is clustered (نضد حمله) from its bottom to its top. Al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): It was recited (وقرئ) as وطلع منضود (and clustered spathes). He said: [Meaning] some of it upon some others (بعضه إلى بعض). In al-Majma', the general populace (العامّة) narrated from Ali (peace be upon him): A man recited in his presence وَطَلْحٍ مَنْضُودٍ ** (and clustered banana trees), so he said: "What is the matter with al-talh (الطّلح)? It is only wa-tala' (وطلع), like His saying: وَنَخْلٍ طَلْعُها هَضِيمٌ (and date-palms with clustered spathes). He was asked: "Will you not change it?" He replied: "Indeed, the Quran is not to be stirred up (لا يهاج) today, nor moved (ولا يحرّك)." His son al-Hasan (peace be upon him) and Qays ibn Sa'd narrated this from him. And our companions (أصحابنا) narrated it from Ya'qub, who said: I said to Abu Abdillah (peace be upon him): وَطَلْحٍ مَنْضُودٍ ** (and clustered banana trees)? He said: "No, but wa-tala'in mandud (وطلع منضود) (and clustered spathes)."

(٣٠) وَظِلٍّ مَمْدُودٍ. (30) and an extended shade,

In al-Majma', in the narration: That in Paradise there is a tree, the rider travels in its shade for a hundred years without traversing its end. Read, if you wish: ** وَظِلٍّ مَمْدُودٍ.** (30) and an extended shade,

He said, and it is also narrated: That the times of Paradise are like the mornings of summer; there is neither heat nor cold in them.

And in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him), from the Prophet (may Allah bless him and his progeny) in a hadith wherein He describes the people of Paradise. He said: And they will enjoy bliss in their Gardens in ظِلٍّ مَمْدُودٍ ** (an extended shade), in the like of what is between the rising of the dawn (طلوع الفجر) and the rising of the sun (طلوع الشمس), and better than that.

footnote (١) And it was said: It is a tree that exists in Yemen and in the Hijaz, among the most beautiful trees in appearance.

(٣١) وَماءٍ مَسْكُوبٍ ** (31) and flowing water, Al-Qummi (said): Meaning, poured/sprinkled (مرشوش). (٣٢) وَفاكِهَةٍ كَثِيرَةٍ. (32) and abundant fruits, (٣٣) لا مَقْطُوعَةٍ وَلا مَمْنُوعَةٍ ** (33) neither intercepted nor forbidden, And no one is prevented from taking them. Al-Qummi narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, who said: "When I entered Paradise, I saw in Paradise the Tuba tree (شجرة طوبى). Its root is in the house of Ali عليه السلام, and there is no palace or dwelling in Paradise except that it has a branch (فنن) from it. Its highest part consists of baskets (أسفاط) of garments (حلل) made of fine silk (سندس) and heavy silk brocade (إستبرق). The believing servant will have a million baskets (ألف ألف سفط), and in every basket, there are a hundred garments (مائة حلّة), none of which resembles another, being of different colors. These are the clothes of the people of Paradise. Its middle part is an extended shade (ظلّ ممدود) spanning the width of Paradise, and the width of Paradise is like the width of the heavens and the earth, prepared for those who believed in Allah and His messengers. A rider travels in that shade for a journey of two hundred years, and he does not cut it off. That is His saying: وَظِلٍّ مَمْدُودٍ ** (30) and an extended shade. And its lowest part consists of the fruits and food of the people of Paradise, hanging down in their houses. In one branch (القضيب) of it, there are a hundred kinds of fruit, some of which you have seen in the abode of the world, and some of which you have not seen, and some of which you have heard of, and some of which you have not heard of. And whenever something is picked from it, another grows in its place, ** لا مَقْطُوعَةٍ وَلا مَمْنُوعَةٍ ** (neither intercepted nor forbidden)." In al-Ihtijaj, narrated from al-Sadiq عليه السلام: He was asked why people say that a man among the people of Paradise comes to a fruit and takes it, and when he eats it, it returns to its original form. He said: "Yes, that is according to the analogy of the lamp (السراج). The one who seeks fire (القابس) comes and takes a flame from it, and nothing is diminished from its light, even though the world has been filled with lamps from it." In al-Basair, narrated from him عليه السلام regarding this Ayah: "By Allah, it is not what people assume; rather, it is the scholar (العالم) and what proceeds from him."

(٣٤) وَفُرُشٍ مَرْفُوعَةٍ ** (34) and elevated couches, some of them above others, made of silk and brocade in different colors, and their stuffing is musk, ambergris, and camphor. This is narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم in the hadith describing Paradise, recorded in al-Kafi and al-Qummi, and it has passed in Surat al-Zumar. And perhaps it is interpreted as women, and their elevation is upon the couches (الأرائك), or in their beauty, or their perfection, based on the evidence of what follows it. It was said: When the state of the Foremost (السّابقين) in blessings was likened to the most perfect thing imaginable for the people of the cities (أهل المدن), the state of the Companions of the Right (أصحاب اليمين) was likened to the most desired thing by the people of the deserts (أهل البوادي), indicating the difference between the two states.

(٣٥) ** إِنَّا أَنْشَأْناهُنَّ إِنْشاءً ** (35) We have produced them by a [special] production,

meaning, We originated them (ابتدأناهنّ) as an origination (ابتداء) without birth (من غير ولادة).

Al-Qummi said: [They are] the wide-eyed maidens (الحور العين) in Paradise (الجنّة).

And narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked from what thing were the wide-eyed maidens (الحور العين) created? He said: From the luminous soil (تربة الجنّة النّورانية) of Paradise (الجنّة).

The narration (الحديث) has already passed in Surat al-Hajj.

(36) فَجَعَلْناهُنَّ أَبْكاراً ** (We have made them virgins) Meaning: perpetually and in every act of coming (إتيان).

And in al-Ihtijaj, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked: How is the Hur (الحوراء) a virgin every time her husband comes to her? He said: She was created from fragrance (الطّيب); no defect (عاهة) afflicts her, nor does any blight (آفة) mix with her body, nor does anything flow in her opening (ثقبها), nor does menstruation (حيض) defile her. Thus, the womb (الرّحم) is adhered (ملتزقة), since there is no passage (مجرى) in it except for the husband (الا حليل).

** عُرُباً ** (37) It was said: [They are] those who are affectionate (متحنّنات) (1) towards their husbands, loving towards them. It is the plural of عروب. And al-Qummi said: They speak in Arabic (بالعربيّة). And in al-Majma', in the narration concerning the virtue of the warriors (الغزاة), narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): He was asked about al-ʿUrūbah (العروبة), and He said: It is the coquettish (الغنجة), the pleasing (الرضيّة), the desirable (الشهيّة). And it was recited with the quiescence (سكون) of the rāʾ (الرّاء).

أَتْراباً ** (38) [They are] peers (لدات) of the same age (على سنّ واحد). Al-Qummi means: having level teeth (مستويات الأسنان).

In al-Majma', narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), in the narration concerning the virtue of the warriors (الغزاة) and the description of Paradise (الجنّة): Upon every couch (سرير) there are forty mattresses (فراشاً), the thickness (غلظ) of every mattress is forty cubits (ذراعاً). Upon every mattress is a wife from the wide-eyed houris (الحور العين), ʿuruban atrāban (عُرباً اتراباً).

And in al-Jawami', narrated from the Prophet (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace): They are those women who were taken [in death] in the abode of the world (دار الدنيا) as old women (عجائز), gray-haired (شمطاء), and bleary-eyed (رمصاء). Allah will make them, after old age (الكبر), peers (اتراباً) of a single birth (ميلاد واحد) in equality (الاستواء). Whenever their husbands approach them (أتاهنّ), they will find them virgins (ابكاراً).

(1) Al-Haneen: longing (الشوق) and intense weeping (شدّة البكاء).

(٣٨) لِأَصْحابِ الْيَمِينِ (38) for the People of the Right Hand. Al-Qummi said: They are the companions of the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him).

(٣٩) ثُلَّةٌ مِنَ الْأَوَّلِينَ (39) A multitude from the former generations, He said: They are from the group (الطبقة) that was with the Prophet (may Allah bless him and his progeny).

(٤٠) وَثُلَّةٌ مِنَ الْآخِرِينَ (40) and a multitude from the latter generations. He said: They are those from this nation (الأمّة) who came after the Prophet (may Allah bless him and his progeny). And narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about them, and He said: ثُلَّةٌ مِنَ الْأَوَّلِينَ ** (A multitude from the former generations) is Hizqil, the believer from the family of Pharaoh, and وَثُلَّةٌ مِنَ الْآخِرِينَ ** (and a multitude from the latter generations) is Ali ibn Abi Talib (peace be upon him). And in al-Majma', narrated from a group of exegetes (المفسّرين), meaning: a group from the past nations (الأمم الماضية) that were before this nation (الأمّة), and a group from the believers of this nation. And narrated from the Prophet (may Allah bless him and his progeny), in a marfu' chain: "Indeed, both multitudes (الثلثين) are entirely from My nation (أمّتي)." Then He supported the first saying by His saying: "Indeed, I hope that you will be half of the People of Paradise," then He recited this Ayah. And in al-Khisal, narrated from Him (may Allah bless him and his progeny): "The People of Paradise are one hundred and twenty rows (صفّاً); this nation (الأمّة) comprises eighty rows of them."

(٤١) وَأَصْحابُ الشِّمالِ ما أَصْحابُ الشِّمالِ. (41) And the People of the Left Hand—what are the People of the Left Hand? (٤٢) فِي سَمُومٍ ** In the heat of a fire that penetrates the pores (المسام). ** وَحَمِيمٍ ** Water that is extreme in heat. (٤٣) وَظِلٍّ مِنْ يَحْمُومٍ ** From black smoke. (٤٤) لا بارِدٍ ** Like other shade ** وَلا كَرِيمٍ ** Nor beneficial. Al-Qummi said: The People of the Left Hand (الشِّمالِ) are the enemies of the Family of Muhammad (peace and blessings of Allah be upon them and their family) and their companions who befriended them. ** فِي سَمُومٍ وَحَمِيمٍ ** He said: al-Samūm (السموم) is the name of the Fire, and al-Hamīm (الحميم) is water that has been heated. ** وَظِلٍّ مِنْ يَحْمُومٍ ** He said: A shade (ظلة) of intense heat. ** لا بارِدٍ وَلا كَرِيمٍ ** He said: It is not good (طيّب).

(٤٥) إِنَّهُمْ كانُوا قَبْلَ ذلِكَ مُتْرَفِينَ ** (45) They had been affluent before that, immersed in desires (الشهوات). (٤٦) وَكانُوا يُصِرُّونَ عَلَى الْحِنْثِ الْعَظِيمِ ** (46) and they used to persist in the great sin, the great sin (الذنب العظيم). It is said that it means polytheism (الشرك). (٤٧) وَكانُوا يَقُولُونَ أَإِذا مِتْنا وَكُنَّا تُراباً وَعِظاماً أَإِنَّا لَمَبْعُوثُونَ. (47) and they used to say, 'When we are dead and become dust and bones, shall we indeed be resurrected? (٤٨) أَوَآباؤُنَا الْأَوَّلُونَ ** (48) What, even our forefathers of yore?' And it was read (وقرئ) with the sukun on the waw (او بالسكون). (٤٩) قُلْ إِنَّ الْأَوَّلِينَ وَالْآخِرِينَ. (49) Say, 'Indeed the former and the latter (٥٠) لَمَجْمُوعُونَ إِلى مِيقاتِ يَوْمٍ مَعْلُومٍ ** (50) will surely be gathered for the tryst of a known day.' [They will be gathered] to what the world (الدنيا) has been appointed by time (وقّت) and limited by a specific day (يوم معيّن) known to Allah. (٥١) ثُمَّ إِنَّكُمْ أَيُّهَا الضَّالُّونَ الْمُكَذِّبُونَ ** (51) Then you, O straying deniers, of the Resurrection (البعث). (٥٢) لَآكِلُونَ مِنْ شَجَرٍ مِنْ زَقُّومٍ. (52) will surely eat of the tree of Zaqqum, (٥٣) فَمالِؤُنَ مِنْهَا الْبُطُونَ ** (53) filling your bellies with it, due to intense hunger (شدّة الجوع). (٥٤) فَشارِبُونَ عَلَيْهِ مِنَ الْحَمِيمِ ** (54) and drinking over it boiling water, due to overwhelming thirst (غلبة العطش). (٥٥) فَشارِبُونَ شُرْبَ الْهِيمِ ** (55) drinking like thirsty camels.' The camels (الإبل) afflicted by al-Hīm (الْهِيمِ), which is a disease resembling dropsy (الاستسقاء), being the plural of ahyam (اهيم) (masculine) and haymā' (هيماء) (feminine). Or [it means] the sands (الرمال), based on the premise that it is the plural of hiyām (هيام) with a fathah (بالفتح), which is sand that does not hold together (لا يتماسك). In al-Faqih, al-Mahasin, and al-Ma'ani, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about al-Hīm (الْهِيمِ), and He said: "The camels (الإبل)." And in a narration: الْهِيمِ ** means the sand (الرمل). And shurb (شرب) was read with the dammah on the shin (بضمّ الشين).

(56) ** هذا نُزُلُهُمْ يَوْمَ الدِّينِ ** (This will be their hospitality on the Day of Retribution.) So what is your opinion concerning what will be for them after they have settled in the Hellfire (الجحيم)? And in it is mockery (تهكّم) of them, because the nuzul (النزل) is what is prepared for the guest (النازل) as an honor (تكرمة) to him. And it was said that the nuzul (النزل) is that upon which its owner descends. Al-Qummi said: This is their reward (ثوابهم) on the Day of Recompense (يوم المجازاة).

(٥٧) نَحْنُ خَلَقْناكُمْ فَلَوْ لا تُصَدِّقُونَ ** (57) We created you, so why do you not believe? (Do you not believe) in the creation (الخلق) or the resurrection (البعث). (٥٨) أَفَرَأَيْتُمْ ما تُمْنُونَ ** (58) Have you considered what you ejaculate? That which you cast into the wombs, consisting of semen drops (النّطف). (٥٩) أَأَنْتُمْ تَخْلُقُونَهُ ** (59) Is it you who create it, Do you make it a complete human being (بشراً سويّاً)? ** أَمْ نَحْنُ الْخالِقُونَ. ** or are We the creators? (٦٠) نَحْنُ قَدَّرْنا بَيْنَكُمُ الْمَوْتَ ** (60) We have apportioned death among you, We have divided it among you and decreed the death of every one at a specific time. And it was read with a lightened dāl (بتخفيف الدّال). ** وَما نَحْنُ بِمَسْبُوقِينَ ** and We will not be outmaneuvered, meaning: We will not be overcome (بِمغلوبين). (٦١) عَلى أَنْ نُبَدِّلَ أَمْثالَكُمْ ** (61) so that We may replace you with others like you, That We replace your likes (اشباهكم) with others, so We create in your stead. وَنُنْشِئَكُمْ فِي ما لا تَعْلَمُونَ and originate you in a way you do not know. In a creation (نشأة) that you do not know. (٦٢) وَلَقَدْ عَلِمْتُمُ النَّشْأَةَ الْأُولى فَلَوْ لا تَذَكَّرُونَ ** (62) Certainly you have known the first creation, so why do you not take admonition? That whoever was capable of the first creation is capable of the other creation (النّشأة الأخرى).

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sajjad (peace be upon him): "The wonder, all the wonder, is for him who denies the other creation (النّشأة الأخرى) while he sees the first creation (النّشأة الأولى)."

(٦٣) أَفَرَأَيْتُمْ ما تَحْرُثُونَ ** (63) Have you considered what you sow? You sow its seed. (٦٤) أَأَنْتُمْ تَزْرَعُونَهُ أَمْ نَحْنُ الزَّارِعُونَ ** (64) Is it you who grow it, or are We the Growers? You cause it to grow, أَمْ نَحْنُ الزَّارِعُونَ ** the Causers of growth. In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: "Let none of you say, 'I grew (زرعت)'; rather, let him say, 'I sowed (حرثت).'" (٦٥) لَوْ نَشاءُ لَجَعَلْناهُ حُطاماً فَظَلْتُمْ تَفَكَّهُونَ ** (65) If We wished, We would turn it into chaff, and you would be left lamenting: dry straw (هشيماً). فَظَلْتُمْ تَفَكَّهُونَ ** you would speak about it, expressing astonishment and regret over what you spent on it. And al-tafakkuh (التفكّه) is the moving between different kinds of fruit (الفاكهة); it has been borrowed to mean moving between different kinds of speech (الحديث). (٦٦) إِنَّا لَمُغْرَمُونَ ** (66) 'We have been penalized! We are certainly obligated to pay the fine (غرامة) for what we spent, or we are ruined due to the destruction of our provision (رزقنا), derived from al-ghurām (الغرام). And it was recited a-innā (أانّا) with interrogation. (٦٧) بَلْ نَحْنُ مَحْرُومُونَ ** (67) Rather, we have been deprived!' are a people مَحْرُومُونَ ** We have been deprived of our provision (رزقنا). (٦٨) أَفَرَأَيْتُمُ الْماءَ الَّذِي تَشْرَبُونَ ** (68) Have you considered the water that you drink? meaning, the fresh water (العذب) suitable for drinking. (٦٩) أَأَنْتُمْ أَنْزَلْتُمُوهُ مِنَ الْمُزْنِ أَمْ نَحْنُ الْمُنْزِلُونَ ** (69) Is it you who send it down from the raincloud, or are We the Sender? from the cloud (السّحاب) أَمْ نَحْنُ الْمُنْزِلُونَ ** by Our power. (٧٠) لَوْ نَشاءُ جَعَلْناهُ أُجاجاً فَلَوْ لا تَشْكُرُونَ ** (70) If We wished, We would make it bitter. So why do you not give thanks? It was said: salty (ملحاً). And al-Qummi (القمّيّ) said: meaning, intensely bitter (زعاقاً). فَلَوْ لا تَشْكُرُونَ ** for examples of these essential blessings (النعم الضرورية). (٧١) أَفَرَأَيْتُمُ النَّارَ الَّتِي تُورُونَ ** (71) Have you considered the fire that you kindle? You strike [the spark] (تقدحون). (٧٢) أَأَنْتُمْ أَنْشَأْتُمْ شَجَرَتَها أَمْ نَحْنُ الْمُنْشِؤُنَ ** (72) Is it you who produced its tree, or are We the Producer? meaning the tree from which the fire-starter (الزّناد) is taken. (٧٣) نَحْنُ جَعَلْناها تَذْكِرَةً وَمَتاعاً لِلْمُقْوِينَ ** (73) We have made it a reminder, and a comfort for the travelers. We made the fire of the fire-starter (نار الزّناد) تَذْكِرَةً . Al-Qummi (القمّيّ) said: for us on the Day of Resurrection (يوم القيامة). And narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "Indeed, this fire of yours is one part of seventy parts of the Fire of Hell (نار جهنّم). It has been extinguished seventy times with water, then it flared up. Were it not for that, no human being (آدميّ) would be able to extinguish it. And indeed, it will be brought on the Day of Resurrection (يوم القيامة) until it is placed upon the [Great] Fire, and it will scream a scream such that no angel brought near, nor prophet sent, will remain except that he will fall upon his knees, terrified by its scream." ** وَمَتاعاً ** and a benefit (منفعة) لِلْمُقْوِينَ ** those who settle in al-quwā' (القواء), which is the barren desert (القفر), or for those whose stomachs or travel provisions (مزاودهم) are empty of food, derived from aqwat al-dār (أقوت الدار) when the dwelling is empty of its inhabitants. This is what was said. And al-Qummi (القمّيّ) said: the needy (المحتاجين).

(٧٤) فَسَبِّحْ بِاسْمِ رَبِّكَ الْعَظِيمِ ** (74) So glorify the Name of your Lord, the Great. So perform glorification (التسبيح) by mentioning His Name. In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله: When this Ayah was revealed, He said: "Place it in your bowing (ركوعكم)." And in al-Faqih: the like of it.

(٧٥) فَلا أُقْسِمُ بِمَواقِعِ النُّجُومِ ** (75) I swear by the setting places of the stars— By their falling places (بمساقطها). And it was read (وقرئ) as بموقع (in the singular). Al-Qummi said its meaning is: فَلا أُقْسِمُ بِمَواقِعِ النُّجُومِ (I swear by the setting places of the stars). And in al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir and al-Sadiq (peace be upon them both): The setting places of the stars (مواقع النجوم) are their pelting stones (رجومها) for the devils (للشياطين). The criminals (المجرمون) used to swear by them, so the Glorified One said: فلا اقسم بها (I do not swear by them). He said [this]. And in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: The people of al-Jahiliyyah (the Age of Ignorance) used to swear by them, so Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said: فَلا أُقْسِمُ بِمَواقِعِ النُّجُومِ . He said: [This means] the matter of the one who swears by them is great (عظم امر من يحلف بها).

(٧٦) وَإِنَّهُ لَقَسَمٌ لَوْ تَعْلَمُونَ عَظِيمٌ. (And indeed that is a great oath, if you knew.) In al-Faqih, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He means by it the oath of disavowal (اليمين بالبراءة) from the Imams (peace be upon them), which a man swears by, that this is great in the sight of Allah. He said: And this hadith is in Nawadir al-Hikmah.

(٧٧) إِنَّهُ لَقُرْآنٌ كَرِيمٌ ** (77) Indeed, it is a noble Qur’ān, [It is] of great benefit because of its inclusion of the fundamental important sciences (أصول العلوم المهمّة) for the rectification of livelihood (المعاش) and the Hereafter (المعاد). (٧٨) فِي كِتابٍ مَكْنُونٍ ** (78) in a preserved Book, [It is] protected (مصون), and it is the Tablet (اللّوح), as mentioned in the hadith interpreting ن وَالْقَلَمِ. (Nūn. By the Pen and what they write). (٧٩) لا يَمَسُّهُ إِلَّا الْمُطَهَّرُونَ ** (79) which none touches except the purified ones. None perceives the Tablet (اللّوح) except the purified ones (المطهرون) from physical impurities (الكدورات الجسمانيّة). Or, none touches the Qur’an except the purified ones (المطهّرون) from ritual impurities (الأحداث), thus being a negation (نفياً) in the meaning of a prohibition (نهي). In al-Tahdhib, narrated from al-Kazim (peace be upon him), he said: "Do not touch the Mushaf (copy of the Qur’an) while you are not in a state of purity (طهر), nor while you are junub (in a state of major ritual impurity). Do not touch its thread (خيطه) nor hang it up. Indeed, Allah the Almighty says: لا يَمَسُّهُ إِلَّا الْمُطَهَّرُونَ (which none touches except the purified ones)." And in al-Ihtijaj: When Umar was appointed Caliph, he asked Ali (peace be upon him) to hand over the Qur’an to them so that they might distort it among themselves. So he said: "O Abu al-Hasan, bring the Qur’an which you brought to Abu Bakr so that we may agree upon it." He (peace be upon him) replied: "Far be it! There is no way to that. I only brought it to Abu Bakr so that the proof (الحجّة) might be established against you, and so that you would not say on the Day of Resurrection, 'Indeed, we were unaware of this,' or say, 'You did not bring it to us.' For the Qur’an which is with me لا يَمَسُّهُ إِلَّا الْمُطَهَّرُونَ ** (which none touches except the purified ones) and the Successors (الأوصياء) from my progeny." Then Umar said: "Is there a known time for its manifestation (إظهاره)?" Ali (peace be upon him) said: "Yes, when the Qā’im (القائم) from my progeny rises, He will manifest it and compel the people to follow it, and the Sunnah (السّنة) will be rewarded through it."

أقولُ : In verification (التحقيق), there is no contradiction (لا مُنافاة) between the two meanings, due to the permissibility of combining them and intending both of them, or that one of them is the exegesis (تفسيراً) and the other is the esoteric interpretation (تأويلاً).

(٨٠) تَنْزِيلٌ مِنْ رَبِّ الْعالَمِينَ. (80) (It is) a sending down from the Lord of all the worlds. (٨١) أَفَبِهذَا الْحَدِيثِ ** meaning the Quran أَنْتُمْ مُدْهِنُونَ ** (81) Is it this discourse that you disdain? (meaning) treating lightly (متهاونون). (٨٢) وَتَجْعَلُونَ رِزْقَكُمْ ** meaning the gratitude for your provision (شكر رزقكم) أَنَّكُمْ تُكَذِّبُونَ ** (82) And make your provision that you deny (it)? meaning: by Him Who sent it down upon you and provided it to you, since you attribute things to the stellar influences (الأنواء).

Al-Qummi narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): He recited Surat al-Waqi'ah and said: تَجْعَلُونَ شكركم أَنَّكُمْ تُكَذِّبُونَ (And you make your gratitude that you deny). When He departed, He said: "I knew that someone would say: 'Why did He recite it this way?' Its recitation is [as follows]: I heard the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم reciting it thus. And they used to say, when it rained: 'We have been rained upon by the influence (نَبْؤ) of such-and-such.' So Allah revealed: وَتَجْعَلُونَ شكركم أَنَّكُمْ تُكَذِّبُونَ (And you make your gratitude that you deny)."

And narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding His saying: وَتَجْعَلُونَ رِزْقَكُمْ ** He said: "Rather, it is تَجْعَلُونَ شكركم (you make your gratitude)."

(٨٣) فَلَوْ لا إِذا بَلَغَتِ الْحُلْقُومَ ** (83) Why, then, when the soul reaches the throat, Meaning: the soul (النّفس). (٨٤) وَأَنْتُمْ حِينَئِذٍ تَنْظُرُونَ ** (84) and you are watching then, The address is to those around the dying person (المحتضر). (٨٥) وَنَحْنُ أَقْرَبُ إِلَيْهِ ** (85) and We are nearer to him To the dying person (المحتضر) مِنْكُمْ وَلكِنْ لا تُبْصِرُونَ. than you are, but you do not perceive. (٨٦) فَلَوْ لا إِنْ كُنْتُمْ غَيْرَ مَدِينِينَ ** (86) Why, then, if you are not subject to requital, Not subject to requital (مجزيين) on the Day of Resurrection (يوم القيامة), or not owned (مملوكين) and subdued (مقهورين). (٨٧) تَرْجِعُونَها ** (87) do you not bring it back, You bring the soul (النفس) back to its abode (مقرّها) إِنْ كُنْتُمْ صادِقِينَ ** if you are truthful? In your denial (تكذيبكم) and your rejection (تعطيلكم). And the meaning is: If you are not owned (مملوكين) and subject to requital (مجزيين), as is indicated by your denial (جحدكم) of the actions of Allah and your rejection (تكذيبكم) of His signs (آياته), then why do you not bring the souls (الأرواح) back to the bodies (الأبدان) after they have reached the throat (الحلقوم)?

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding this Ayah, He said: "When it (the soul) reaches the throat (الحلقوم), its station in Paradise (الجنّة) is shown to it. So it says: 'Return me to the world (الدنيا) so that I may inform my family of what I see.' It is said to him: 'There is no way (سبيل) to that.'"

(٨٨) فَأَمَّا إِنْ كانَ مِنَ الْمُقَرَّبِينَ ** (88) But if he is one of the Outstrippers, meaning, if the deceased was one of the foremost (السّابقين). (٨٩) فَرَوْحٌ ** (89) then [there will be] repose, then for him is rest (استراحة). And it was read with the dammah on the rāʾ (بضمّ الرّاء). And in al-Majmaʿ, he attributed this reading to the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله and al-Baqir عليه السلام, and it was interpreted as mercy and everlasting life. وَرَيْحانٌ ** and good provision (رزق طيّب). وَجَنَّةُ نَعِيمٍ ** and a Garden of bliss. possessing enjoyment (ذات تنعّم).

In al-Amali and al-Qummi, narrated from al-Sadiq عليه السلام, he said: فَرَوْحٌ وَرَيْحانٌ ** means in his grave. وَجَنَّةُ نَعِيمٍ ** means in the Hereafter.

(٩٠) وَأَمَّا إِنْ كانَ مِنْ أَصْحابِ الْيَمِينِ. (90) But if he is one of the People of the Right Hand, (٩١) فَسَلامٌ لَكَ ** يا صاحب اليمين ** مِنْ أَصْحابِ الْيَمِينِ ** (91) then 'Peace be to you' from the People of the Right Hand. meaning, from your brothers (إخوانك) who greet you with peace (يسلّمون عليك). This is how it was said (كذا قيل).

And al-Qummi (said): It means whoever was among the companions (أصحاب) of the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him). ** فَسَلامٌ لَكَ ** (then 'Peace be to you'), O Muhammad, ** مِنْ أَصْحابِ الْيَمِينِ ** (from the People of the Right Hand), meaning that they will not be punished (ان لا يعذّبوا).

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said to Ali (peace be upon him): "O Ali, they are your followers (شيعتك). So, protect your children from them, lest they kill them."

(٩٢) ** وَأَمَّا إِنْ كانَ مِنَ الْمُكَذِّبِينَ الضَّالِّينَ ** (92) But if he is one of the deniers, the astray,

Meaning the Companions of the Left Hand (أصحاب الشمال). He only described them by their actions as a deterrent against them, and as an indication of what necessitated for them that which He promised them (of punishment).

And in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) in a ḥadīth: "So, these are polytheists (مشركون)." And al-Qummi (said they are) the enemies of the Family of Muhammad (may the blessings of Allah be upon them).

(٩٣) فَنُزُلٌ مِنْ حَمِيمٍ. (93) as hospitality with boiling water, (٩٤) وَتَصْلِيَةُ جَحِيمٍ. (94) and the scorching of hell. In al-Amali and al-Qummi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): فَنُزُلٌ مِنْ حَمِيمٍ ** (as hospitality with boiling water) means in his grave, and وَتَصْلِيَةُ جَحِيمٍ ** (and the scorching of hell) means in the Hereafter. (٩٥) إِنَّ هذا ** (95) This is indeed meaning that which was mentioned in the Surah or concerning the matter of the groups (الفرق), لَهُوَ حَقُّ الْيَقِينِ ** (the certain truth) meaning the truth of the certain news (حق الخبر اليقين). (٩٦) فَسَبِّحْ بِاسْمِ رَبِّكَ الْعَظِيمِ ** (96) So glorify the Name of your Lord, the Great. So exalt Him by mentioning His Name, [purifying Him] from that which is unfitting for the greatness of His status. In Thawab al-A'mal, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Whoever recites Surah al-Waqi'ah every night before he sleeps shall meet Allah, the Mighty and Majestic (عزّ وجلّ), and his face will be like the moon on the night of the full moon. And in al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace): Whoever recites it (the Surah) every night shall never be afflicted by destitution (فاقة) أبداً.
